info.name: Expense Splitter - Automated E2E
info.description:
Automated Postman collection for the Expense Splitter API.

This collection is designed to run end-to-end against the application, capture dynamic IDs, build transaction payloads from created participants, execute both basic and simplified settlement flows, and clean up created data.

How to read this collection:
- Each folder represents a business flow.
- Each request contains endpoint-level documentation in its Description field.
- Dynamic IDs are captured in test scripts and reused by later requests.
- All URLs intentionally use {{baseUrl}} and related Postman variables so the same collection can run on different machines.

item.name: 01 - Participant Management Flow

request.description:
### Purpose
Create a new trip used by the participant-management flow.

### Endpoint
`POST {{baseUrl}}/trip`

### Request body
- `name` — human-readable trip name.
- `participants` — array of participant names to create inside the trip.

### Successful response
Returns the created trip object.
- `tripId` — server-generated unique identifier for the trip.
- `tripName` — persisted trip name.
- `participants` — array of participant objects:
  - `name` — participant display name.
  - `participantId` — server-generated participant identifier used by downstream APIs.

### Notes
This request captures `tripId` for later participant add/remove/detail/delete operations.

exec: pm.test('Participant list now has four entries', function () { pm.expect(data.participants).to.have.length(4); });
pm.test('Added participant Kiran is present', function () { pm.expect(added).to.exist; pm.expect(added.participantId).to.be.a('string').and.not.empty; });
if (!participantFlowTripId) { throw new Error('participant_flow_trip_id is missing'); }

request.description:
### Purpose
Add new participant(s) to an existing trip.

### Endpoint
`POST {{baseUrl}}/trip/{{participant_flow_trip_id}}/participants`

### Path variables
- `participant_flow_trip_id` — trip identifier captured from the create-trip response.

### Request body
- `participants` — array of participant names to add.

### Successful response
Returns the updated trip object.
- `tripId` — unchanged trip identifier.
- `tripName` — trip name.
- `participants` — full current participant list after insertion.

### Notes
This request captures the generated participant ID for `Kiran` so that the removal request can delete the exact participant by ID.

exec: if (!participantFlowTripId) { throw new Error('participant_flow_trip_id is missing'); }

request.description:
### Purpose
Fetch the current state of a trip.

### Endpoint
`GET {{baseUrl}}/trip/{{participant_flow_trip_id}}/details`

### Path variables
- `participant_flow_trip_id` — target trip identifier.

### Successful response
Returns the trip object.
- `tripId`
- `tripName`
- `participants[]`
  - `name`
  - `participantId`

### Notes
Used here as a verification step after participant insertion.

exec: pm.test('Participant list is back to three', function () { pm.expect(data.participants).to.have.length(3); });
pm.test('Kiran is absent after deletion', function () {
if (!participantFlowTripId) { throw new Error('participant_flow_trip_id is missing'); }
if (!participantKiran) { throw new Error('participant_Kiran is missing'); }

request.description:
### Purpose
Remove participant(s) from a trip.

### Endpoint
`DELETE {{baseUrl}}/trip/{{participant_flow_trip_id}}/participants`

### Path variables
- `participant_flow_trip_id` — target trip identifier.

### Request body
- `participants` — array of participant IDs to remove.

### Successful response
Returns the updated trip object after deletion.
- `tripId`
- `tripName`
- `participants[]`

### Notes
The API expects participant IDs, not participant names, for removal.

exec: pm.test('Deleted trip id is echoed back', function () { pm.expect(data.tripId).to.eql(tripId); });
if (!participantFlowTripId) { throw new Error('participant_flow_trip_id is missing'); }

request.description:
### Purpose
Delete the participant-management trip after verification.

### Endpoint
`DELETE {{baseUrl}}/trip/{{participant_flow_trip_id}}`

### Path variables
- `participant_flow_trip_id` — trip identifier to delete.

### Successful response
Returns a minimal acknowledgement payload.
- `tripId` — deleted trip identifier.

### Notes
This is cleanup so the test run leaves no residual data for this flow.

item.description: Flow that verifies trip creation, participant addition, participant removal, detail retrieval, and cleanup for a small trip.

item.name: 02 - Settlement E2E Flow

exec: pm.test('Participant id captured for ' + name, function () { pm.expect(mapByName[name]).to.be.a('string').and.not.empty; });

request.description:
### Purpose
Create the main trip used for settlement, transaction, and cleanup flow verification.

### Endpoint
`POST {{baseUrl}}/trip`

### Request body
- `name` — trip name.
- `participants` — array of participant names.

### Successful response
Returns the created trip object.
- `tripId`
- `tripName`
- `participants[]`
  - `name`
  - `participantId`

### Notes
This request captures all participant IDs because downstream transaction posting must reference participant IDs, not names.

exec: pm.test('Trip id matches the created trip', function () { pm.expect(data.tripId).to.eql(tripId); });
if (!tripId) { throw new Error('trip_id is missing'); }

request.description:
### Purpose
Verify that the settlement-flow trip exists and contains the expected participants.

### Endpoint
`GET {{baseUrl}}/trip/{{trip_id}}/details`

### Path variables
- `trip_id` — target trip identifier.

### Successful response
Returns the current trip object.
- `tripId`
- `tripName`
- `participants[]`

### Notes
Used as a guard step before transactions are posted.

exec: if (!tripId) { throw new Error('trip_id is missing before posting transactions'); }
return pm.collectionVariables.get('participant_' + name) || pm.environment.get('participant_' + name) || pm.globals.get('participant_' + name);
if (missing.length) { throw new Error('Missing participant ids for: ' + missing.join(', ')); }
benefittedBy: [id('Alice'), id('Bob'), id('Smith'), id('Wonder'), id('Mat'), id('Philips'), id('Aleena'), id('Kimberly')]
benefittedBy: [id('Alice'), id('Bob'), id('Smith'), id('Wonder'), id('Mat'), id('Philips'), id('Aleena'), id('Kimberly')]
if (pm.response.code !== 200) { console.log('Post dynamic transactions error payload:', data); }
pm.test('All transaction ids are captured', function () {
var txId = pm.collectionVariables.get('transaction_' + n + '_id') || pm.environment.get('transaction_' + n + '_id') || pm.globals.get('transaction_' + n + '_id');

request.description:
### Purpose
Create multiple transactions in one call using IDs captured from the trip creation response.

### Endpoint
`POST {{baseUrl}}/trip/{{trip_id}}/transactions`

### Path variables
- `trip_id` — target trip identifier.

### Request body
- `transactions` — array of transaction objects. Each transaction contains:
  - `spentAmount` — numeric amount spent.
  - `spentBy` — participant ID of the payer.
  - `spentOn` — expense category enum such as `FOOD`, `TRANSPORT`, or `STAY`.
  - `spentDate` — date string expected by the backend.
  - `benefittedBy` — array of participant IDs who share this transaction.

### Successful response
Returns the created transaction list.
Each element contains:
- `transactionId` — unique transaction identifier.
- `benefittedBy[]` — participant objects resolved by the backend.
- `amount` or `spentAmount` depending on controller mapping.
- `shareType` — split type, typically `EQUAL`.
- `spentBy` — participant object representing payer.
- `spentDate`
- `spentOn`

### Notes
The test scripts capture all four transaction IDs for later deletion and recomputation checks.

exec: pm.test('Settlement response is an array', function () { pm.expect(responseJson).to.be.an('array'); });
pm.test(`Basic settlement row ${index + 1} is well formed`, function () {
if (!tripId) { throw new Error('trip_id is missing'); }

request.description:
### Purpose
Compute the non-simplified settlement for the trip.

### Endpoint
`POST {{baseUrl}}/trip/{{trip_id}}/settlement?simplify=false`

### Query parameters
- `simplify=false` — asks the backend for the basic settlement mode.

### Successful response
Returns a settlement array (or an object containing such an array depending on controller shape).
Each settlement row contains:
- `from` — debtor participant ID.
- `to` — creditor participant ID.
- `amount` — amount to transfer.

### Notes
This step stores the number of rows so the simplified settlement can be checked against it.

exec: pm.test('Settlement response is an array', function () { pm.expect(responseJson).to.be.an('array'); });
pm.test('Simplified settlement is not longer than the basic settlement', function () { pm.expect(responseJson.length).to.be.at.most(basicCount); });
if (!tripId) { throw new Error('trip_id is missing'); }

request.description:
### Purpose
Compute the simplified settlement for the trip.

### Endpoint
`POST {{baseUrl}}/trip/{{trip_id}}/settlement?simplify=true`

### Query parameters
- `simplify=true` — asks the backend for simplified settlement mode.

### Successful response
Returns settlement rows with the same semantic shape as the basic settlement:
- `from`
- `to`
- `amount`

### Notes
The collection asserts that the simplified settlement is not longer than the basic settlement.

exec: pm.test('Deleted transaction id matches the stored id', function () { pm.expect(data.transactionId).to.eql(txId); });
if (!tripId) { throw new Error('trip_id is missing'); }
if (!txId) { throw new Error('transaction_1_id is missing'); }

request.description:
### Purpose
Delete the first created transaction to verify recomputation and cleanup.

### Endpoint
`DELETE {{baseUrl}}/trip/{{trip_id}}/transactions/{{transaction_1_id}}`

### Path variables
- `trip_id` — trip identifier.
- `transaction_1_id` — captured transaction identifier.

### Successful response
Returns acknowledgement payload:
- `transactionId` — deleted transaction identifier.

exec: pm.test('Settlement after deletion is still an array', function () { pm.expect(responseJson).to.be.an('array'); });
if (!tripId) { throw new Error('trip_id is missing'); }

request.description:
### Purpose
Verify that settlement can be recomputed after a transaction is removed.

### Endpoint
`POST {{baseUrl}}/trip/{{trip_id}}/settlement?simplify=false`

### Successful response
Returns settlement rows again.
- `from`
- `to`
- `amount`

### Notes
This confirms that settlement is recomputed from persisted transaction state rather than relying on stale in-memory results.

exec: pm.test('Deleted transaction id matches the stored id', function () { pm.expect(data.transactionId).to.eql(txId); });
if (!tripId) { throw new Error('trip_id is missing'); }
if (!txId) { throw new Error('transaction_2_id is missing'); }

request.description:
### Purpose
Delete the second created transaction during cleanup.

### Endpoint
`DELETE {{baseUrl}}/trip/{{trip_id}}/transactions/{{transaction_2_id}}`

### Successful response
- `transactionId` — deleted transaction identifier.

exec: pm.test('Deleted transaction id matches the stored id', function () { pm.expect(data.transactionId).to.eql(txId); });
if (!tripId) { throw new Error('trip_id is missing'); }
if (!txId) { throw new Error('transaction_3_id is missing'); }

request.description:
### Purpose
Delete the third created transaction during cleanup.

### Endpoint
`DELETE {{baseUrl}}/trip/{{trip_id}}/transactions/{{transaction_3_id}}`

### Successful response
- `transactionId` — deleted transaction identifier.

exec: pm.test('Deleted transaction id matches the stored id', function () { pm.expect(data.transactionId).to.eql(txId); });
if (!tripId) { throw new Error('trip_id is missing'); }
if (!txId) { throw new Error('transaction_4_id is missing'); }

request.description:
### Purpose
Delete the fourth created transaction during cleanup.

### Endpoint
`DELETE {{baseUrl}}/trip/{{trip_id}}/transactions/{{transaction_4_id}}`

### Successful response
- `transactionId` — deleted transaction identifier.

exec: pm.test('Deleted trip id is echoed back', function () { pm.expect(data.tripId).to.eql(tripId); });
if (!tripId) { throw new Error('trip_id is missing'); }

request.description:
### Purpose
Delete the settlement-flow trip after all transactions have been cleaned up.

### Endpoint
`DELETE {{baseUrl}}/trip/{{trip_id}}`

### Path variables
- `trip_id` — trip identifier to delete.

### Successful response
Returns acknowledgement payload:
- `tripId` — deleted trip identifier.

### Notes
This request is executed only after all transactions have been deleted so the backend deletion constraints are respected.

item.description: Flow that creates a full trip, posts transactions, computes both settlement modes, verifies recomputation after deletion, and performs complete cleanup.

item.name: 03 - Negative Validation Flow
Create trip with blank name should fail

exec: pm.test('Validation error contains the name field', function () { pm.expect(data.name).to.eql('Trip name cannot be blank.'); });

request.description:
### Purpose
Verify request-body validation for blank trip names.

### Endpoint
`POST {{baseUrl}}/trip`

### Request body
- `name` — intentionally blank.
- `participants` — minimal valid participant list.

### Expected error response
Returns validation error payload.
Typical fields:
- `name` — error message for the invalid trip name field.

### Purpose
Create a minimal trip used for negative transaction validation.

### Endpoint
`POST {{baseUrl}}/trip`

### Request body
- `name`
- `participants`

### Successful response
Returns the created trip and captures `negative_trip_id` for later invalid-transaction and cleanup requests.

exec: pm.test('Validation error contains benefittedBy', function () { pm.expect(data['transactions[0].benefittedBy']).to.eql('You must provide at least one benefitted by.'); });
if (!negativeTripId) { throw new Error('negative_trip_id is missing'); }

request.description:
### Purpose
Verify validation for invalid transaction payloads.

### Endpoint
`POST {{baseUrl}}/trip/{{negative_trip_id}}/transactions`

### Request body
Contains one intentionally invalid transaction:
- `benefittedBy` is empty.

### Expected error response
Returns validation payload.
Typical field:
- `transactions[0].benefittedBy` — validation message describing why the array is invalid.

exec: if (!negativeTripId) { throw new Error('negative_trip_id is missing'); }

request.description:
### Purpose
Cleanup request for the negative-flow trip.

### Endpoint
`DELETE {{baseUrl}}/trip/{{negative_trip_id}}`

### Successful response
Returns acknowledgement that the trip was removed.

item.description: Flow that verifies validation and error contracts without affecting the main happy-path flow.

item.name: 99 - Transaction Get APIs
Get all transactions in a trip

request.description:
### Purpose
Reserved for future use once the transaction list GET API is fully supported.

### Intended endpoint
`GET {{baseUrl}}/trip/{{trip_id}}/transactions`

### Intended response
Array of transaction objects:
- `transactionId`
- `benefittedBy`
- `spentBy`
- `spentOn`
- `spentDate`
- `shareType`
- `amount`

### Current status
This request is documented but not part of the active green automation lane.

### Purpose
Reserved for future use once the single-transaction GET API is fully supported.

### Intended endpoint
`GET {{baseUrl}}/trip/{{trip_id}}/transactions/{{transaction_1_id}}`

### Intended response
Single transaction object:
- `transactionId`
- `benefittedBy`
- `spentBy`
- `spentOn`
- `spentDate`
- `shareType`
- `amount`

### Current status
This request is documented but not part of the active green automation lane.

item.description: Documented but non-critical requests intended for future activation once the corresponding controller behavior is fully stable.
